data.title: Hello, world from .NET 9
data.excerpt: A blog post stored as a JSON file in @webhouse/cms and rendered by an ASP.NET Core 9 Razor Page.
data.content:
## The setup

This post lives at `content/posts/hello-world.json`. It's a flat JSON file in the @webhouse/cms format. **ASP.NET Core reads it directly** via the `WebhouseReader` service — no API calls, no database, just `Directory.EnumerateFiles()`.

## Why this matters

The content is in your git repository. The schema is in `cms.config.ts`. The renderer is your .NET app. There's no vendor lock-in: if you ever want to swap out @webhouse/cms admin, your content stays put.

## What you can edit

Open CMS admin and point it at this folder. Edit the title above. Save. Reload this page in your browser. The change is live.

That's the whole story.
data.author: Christian Broberg
